metadata.title:
GORDON TELLS WHAT


HE PLEASES TO TELL

pages.raw_text:
Having arranged, through his at
torney, the sort Of an examination
he would be expected to undergo,
M. j. dordon has appeared before
the Root Gordon investigation COM
mission finally at his own pleasure
and convenience. AS reported from
Seattle, the main condition of his
condescending was that he was not
to be. asked anything about his
relations with the Great Northern,
which from the outside appears to
have shut of any inquiry into that
corporations corrupt practices in
this state If, as generally sur
miseda 1t has engaged. in corrupt
practices.


What Gordon told the commits
sion oatside of this is still secret,
but the results of all the, investi-
gatings that has kept the state on
keeni Edge since the matter first
became public is promised shortly
